name: Research Ethics Taxonomy Class Hierarchy
input: Create a class diagram showing the taxonomy of research ethics concepts. Base class: EthicalPrinciple (attributes: name, description; methods: evaluate(), report_violation()). Subclasses: Beneficence (attributes: risk_benefit_ratio; methods: assess_harm(), assess_benefit()), NonMaleficence (attributes: harm_threshold; methods: identify_risks()), Autonomy (attributes: consent_type; methods: verify_informed_consent(), assess_capacity()), Justice (attributes: selection_criteria; methods: evaluate_fair_selection(), assess_burden_distribution()). Additional class: IRBReview (attributes: protocol_id, review_type, committee_members; methods: full_board_review(), expedited_review(), exempt_determination()). IRBReview uses all EthicalPrinciple subclasses for evaluation. Show inheritance from EthicalPrinciple to its 4 subclasses.
qualityCriteria: All 6 classes present (EthicalPrinciple, Beneficence, NonMaleficence, Autonomy, Justice, IRBReview)
Inheritance relationships shown (4 subclasses extend EthicalPrinciple)
Attributes and methods present in each class
No truncated class names (NonMaleficence fully spelled)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: UML class hierarchy with inheritance arrows pointing from subclasses to base class, IRBReview connected via association, all compartments visible.
